title: Add graceful signal handling
description: Introduce SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers that make in-flight writes atomic and clean up resources before exiting.
body: Steps: 1. Inventory resources the tool holds mid-run: open output files, temp files, child processes, locks. 2. Make output atomic: write to a temp file and rename on success so an interrupt never leaves a partial real file. 3. Install SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers that flush, remove temp files, and terminate/wait on children. 4. Exit with 128+signal so callers can detect interruption. 5. Test by interrupting mid-run and inspecting the filesystem and process table. Rollback point: before changing output to temp+rename (verify consumers tolerate it). Validation: an interrupted run leaves no partial files and no orphaned children; ignoring signals or writing in place is the failure this prevents.
